column2:
Instructions:
1. Fill in the name of the team.
2. Enter the skills that are required for the team and briefly describe each skill.
3. Rate the skill importance on a scale from 1 to 5 based on the 1st table on the right-hand side.
4. Rate the skill level required on a scale from 1 to 5 based on the 2nd table on the right-hand side.

column3: Strategic thinking / Problem solving / Market Value Composition /Decision-making / Critical Thinking  /Numerical reasoning
Ownership / Allocation of Resources / KPIs setting & monitoring / Identification of needs & project design
column8: Not Important: Peripheral to the job role; a 'nice-to-have' but not essential.
column11: Novice: Limited understanding of the skill. Requires significant guidance and is in the early stages of learning.

column3: Performance analysis & evaluation / SWOT analysis / Productivity metrics / People analytics / Workforce analysis
column8: Of Minor Importance: Occasionally useful, but not central; enhances some tasks.
column11: Basic: Has foundational knowledge but lacks hands-on experience. Can perform simple tasks with some assistance.

column3: Stakeholders' management / Conflict management / Resilience / Adaptive to change / Flexibility
column8: Moderately Important: Regularly used; expected for the role.
column11: Competent: Comfortable with the skill. Can perform tasks independently but may require occasional guidance for complex challenges.

column3: Ability to inspire and motivate /  Effective communication / Constructive Feedback / Talent management
column8: Very Important: Frequently used; absence hinders performance; defining for the role.
column11: Proficient: Strong understanding with extensive hands-on experience. Can handle complex tasks and resolve issues effectively.

column8: Critically Important: Indispensable for primary responsibilities; must-have for the job.
column11: Expert: Mastery of the skill. Recognized as a go-to person for challenges and can guide or train others.
